evals.name: ref-vs-source-conventions
prompt: Scan all .sql models under models/.
For each file:
(a) reads from other dbt models or seeds must use ref(), not hardcoded database.schema.table.
(b) reads from raw/external tables that belong in sources must use source(), not ref().
List each model: pass/fail, with quoted evidence (snippet or line).
If the project has no sources.yml yet, say so and still flag any raw SQL that should become source() once defined.
expected_output: A short audit table (or bullet list) mapping each model to whether ref/source conventions are satisfied,

assertions.text: Review states that internal dbt models and seeds must be referenced with ref(), not bare warehouse identifiers.
Review states that external/raw warehouse tables (outside the dbt project graph) must use source() once declared in YAML, not ref().
Any reported violation names the model file and includes concrete evidence (SQL snippet, pattern, or line-level pointer).

evals.name: validate_data_product_yml
evals.description: Ensure the model has a defined schema contract
prompt: Create a yml file for all the models in the right format: use dbt schema YAML (version 2)
so every model under models/ is listed with descriptions and columns where applicable.
Enable model contracts with enforced: true for models that expose a stable column set
(per dbt contract syntax for your dbt version).
expected_output: One or more valid schema YAML files covering each SQL model;
contract blocks include enforced: true where contracts apply.

assertions.text: YAML for models contains the literal 'enforced: true' (schema contract enforcement).

evals.name: model-line-count-under-200
evals.description: Keep dbt models small and readable
prompt: Scan every .sql file under models/.
Count the number of lines in each file (include blank lines and comments).
Each model must have strictly fewer than 200 lines.
List each model: pass/fail, line count, and path.
For any model at or above 200 lines, recommend how to split or simplify it.
expected_output: A table or bullet list of every model under models/ with its line count and pass/fail status.
Failures name the file, exact line count, and why it exceeds the 200-line limit.

assertions.text: Review states that each dbt model .sql file under models/ must have fewer than 200 lines.
Every model .sql under models/ has strictly fewer than 200 lines.

evals.name: model-schema-yaml-pairing
evals.description: Every dbt model must have a schema YAML file in the same folder
prompt: Scan every .sql file under models/.
For each model, check that a matching schema file exists in the same directory:
either <model_name>.yml or <model_name>.yaml (same basename as the .sql file).
Example: models/staging/stg_customer_sales.sql requires
models/staging/stg_customer_sales.yml or models/staging/stg_customer_sales.yaml.
List each model: pass/fail, and the expected schema file path.
expected_output: A table or bullet list of every model under models/ with pass/fail status.
Failures name the .sql file and the missing .yml or .yaml path.

assertions.text: Review states that every .sql model under models/ must have a matching .yml or .yaml file in the same folder with the same basename.
Every model .sql under models/ has a co-located .yml or .yaml file with the same basename.

evals.name: model-description-from-requirements
evals.description: Every model and macro must document its purpose aligned with the requirements .md file
prompt: Scan every .sql file under models/ and macros/.
For each file, read the matching requirements doc:
docs/staging/<name>.md for models in models/staging/,
docs/marts/<name>.md for models in models/marts/,
docs/macros/<name>.md for files in macros/.
Check that the file has a description that explains its purpose (why it exists),
grounded in the requirements doc (especially the Overview section).
For models, the description should live in the co-located schema .yml/.yaml (models[].description)
For macros, the description should be in a leading SQL comment block at the top of the .sql file.
Reject descriptions that only repeat the file name or list columns without purpose.
List each file: pass/fail, where the description was found, and brief evidence.
expected_output: A table or bullet list of every .sql file under models/ and macros/ with pass/fail status.
Passes quote the description and tie it to the requirements doc Overview (or equivalent).
Failures name the .sql file, the requirements doc path, and what is missing or inadequate.

assertions.text: Review states that every .yml or .yaml file under models/ and every .sql macro under macros/ must have a purpose description derived from its requirements .md file under docs/staging/, docs/marts/, or docs/macros/.
Review reads the paired docs/<layer>/<name>.md requirements file before judging the description.
Review flags descriptions that only restate the file name or column list without explaining purpose.
Every .yml or .yaml file under models/ and every .sql file under macros/ has a non-empty purpose description aligned with its requirements .md file.
